# Series Plan

The series overview and index. Per-story detail lives in [`books/`](books/) — one file per planned story. Update individual book files there; keep this file for the series-level frame.

Lock decisions here deliberately and early — the ones below are the questions every multi-story universe eventually has to answer, and answering them late means retconning.

---

## Series Overview

[Two or three sentences: the shape of the whole series. What's the through-line across stories — a reputation arc, an escalating threat, a slow reveal?]

**Structure:** [What every story shares: length, internal structure, timespan.]

**Setting:** [The canvas, and how much of it each story uses.]

## Locked Decisions

| Decision | Detail |
|----------|--------|
| Story order | [e.g., which character or thread anchors each book, in order] |
| Timeline progression | [e.g., one in-world month per book, Oct → Mar] |
| Structure | [chapters per story, act shape, pacing rules] |
| [Reveal sequencing] | [e.g., whose secret pays off when — the biggest reveal goes last] |
| [Continuity model] | [e.g., loose connectivity: minor characters recur, no serialized "big bad"] |
| Future stories (N+) | [what's allowed to change — cast rotation, time jumps] |

### Character Secret Progression

Track every main-cast secret across the series: when it's introduced, when it cracks open, when it fully pays off. A secret revealed on an unplanned schedule is the most common way a series contradicts itself.

| Character | Secret | Introduced | Cracked Open | Fully Revealed |
|-----------|--------|-----------|-------------|----------------|
| [Name] | [The secret] | Book [N] (hints) | Book [N] | Book [N] |

### World Evolution

[What changes in the world across the series independent of any one plot — what opens, what closes, what shifts. The cast isn't just having adventures; they're witnessing their world change.]
